codeReview.summary: The obs-trace-waterfall feature provides a trace waterfall data structure for task/agent execution visualization. It includes TraceSpan with hierarchical parent-child relationships, SpanKind classification (Task, Agent, Tool, Decision, Llm), TraceSummary with tool duration breakdown, and is_decision_point flag for agent decisions. The feature has 19 comprehensive tests covering all functionality. The fix for opentelemetry-full blocking issues is in separate commits (ab1718e, 9da0572) which properly wire OTLP export and span creation during LLM calls.

issues.description: GenAiSpanBuilder struct is marked #[allow(dead_code)] and is never used. The LLM backends create spans directly via tracer.span_builder() and tracer.build() instead. While this works, GenAiSpanBuilder is dead code that could be removed or properly integrated.

fixAdequacy: adequate - The three original blocking issues from opentelemetry-full have been addressed by separate commits: (1) OtelConfig::from_env() is called at daemon startup in commit 9da0572, (2) Spans are now created during LLM calls in both anthropic.rs and openai.rs via commit ab1718e, (3) init_tracer_provider() sets up the tracer provider with OTLP export. The obs-trace-waterfall feature itself is a separate data structure for trace visualization with 19 passing tests.
